title: Hyperswitch
description: Open-source, Rust-based payment switch and orchestration platform for routing, retrying and reconciling payments across 100+ processors.
installation: Hyperswitch is a multi-container stack. This template deploys the PRIMARY API server, the `hyperswitch-router` (the `standalone` image is the all-in-one self-host variant that bundles config + migrations). It listens on HTTP 8080 (health check: GET /health). Add-ons: a Postgres add-on (the system of record) and a Valkey/Redis add-on (used for caching, locking and as the scheduler/queue backend) are provisioned. CONFIG: Hyperswitch is normally driven by a TOML file, but every value can be overridden with `ROUTER__<SECTION>__<KEY>` env vars (double underscores) - that is what this template uses to wire the add-ons. The router reads both a MASTER and a REPLICA database config; both are pointed at the same Postgres add-on here. COMPANION SERVICES to deploy as their own Kubero apps, each with the SAME database + redis env as this router: (1) SCHEDULER PRODUCER -> image `juspaydotin/hyperswitch-producer:standalone` (enqueues scheduled tasks like retries/webhooks); (2) SCHEDULER CONSUMER -> image `juspaydotin/hyperswitch-consumer:standalone` (executes those tasks); both are required for retries, payment-method-tokenization expiry and outgoing webhooks to work. (3) CONTROL CENTER (admin dashboard) -> image `juspaydotin/hyperswitch-control-center:latest` (HTTP 9000); set its api_url to this router's {{KUBERO_APP_URL}}. (4) CHECKOUT/SDK WEB -> image `juspaydotin/hyperswitch-web:latest` (the unified client-side payment SDK, served on 9050/9060). FIRST RUN: the standalone router auto-applies DB migrations on boot; create your first merchant/admin-api-key via the /user/signup or admin APIs. SECRETS to ROTATE before going live: replace the Postgres password and set strong values for `ROUTER__SECRETS__ADMIN_API_KEY`, `ROUTER__SECRETS__MASTER_ENC_KEY` (a 32-byte hex key, `openssl rand -hex 32`) and `ROUTER__SECRETS__JWT_SECRET`. KMS/managed-encryption and an external message queue are optional and NOT provisioned here.
